titleStmt.title: Letter from Edward O'Farrell to Jean Louis Loiseleur des Longchamps, 4 January 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Edward O'Farrell

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Letter to the French Consul, Jean Louis Loiseleur des Longchamps (1872-1969) requesting information about the result of an application by three men who wished to volunteer for the French Army.Monsieur Jean Louis Amable Loiseleur des Longchamps-Deville served as the French Consul in Ireland from 1913.Sir Edward O'Farrell (c.1857-1926) served as the Assistant Under Secretary to the Lord Lieutenant of Ireland, Sir Ivor Guest Churchill, Lord Wimborne.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
4th January, 1916. No. 29. Sir, With reference to the cases of the three Alsatians,
Louis Herr, Romain Willman n and Joseph Cheray, who
volunteered for the French Army, I am directed by the Lord
Lieutenant to request that you will be so good as to state,
for His Excellency's information, what has been the
result of the applications made by these men. I am,
Sir,
Your obedient Servant, EOFarrell Monsieur Loiseleur des Longchamps, French Consul, 8 Leinster Street, Dublin.
